methodsAttempted.query: site:vsb.cz ("artificial intelligence" OR "generative AI" OR ChatGPT OR "umela inteligence" OR "generativni") (policy OR guidance OR "academic integrity" OR plagiarism OR "AI tools") "VSB"
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found official VSB results including the managed regulations index, AI webinar/event pages, course pages, and an official faculty PDF.

methodsAttempted.query: site:vsb.cz "Rules and recommendations for the use of artificial intelligence" "VSB"
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search confirmed the official regulations index and Central Library page referencing TUO_LEG_24_002.
Firecrawl search surfaced the official Central Library literature-search page and several rejected blog, thesis, and event-style pages.
The main /robots.txt request returned the site 404 page rather than crawl directives; selected pages carried index/follow metadata and Firecrawl opened them publicly.
Direct unauthenticated curl against the PDF URL looped through the university SSO gateway, but Firecrawl retrieved the same public official PDF from the official document system without credentials.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found VSB-TUO's managed regulations index and the current Czech version B PDF of TUO_LEG_24_002, plus an official Central Library literature-search page that points back to the AI rules and gives AI search guidance. Webinar/event pages, course-specific pages, and an older English version A PDF were not used as primary policy evidence.

stopConditions: Stop after current official AI-specific policy and official adjacent library guidance are captured.
Reject event/news/course-specific items unless they contain policy text with institution-wide scope.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Internal regulations and other regulations of VSB-TUO
Pravidla a doporuceni pro vyuzivani AI na VSB-TUO

artifacts.claimText: VSB-TUO's official managed documents include a current Czech document titled 'Pravidla a doporuceni pro vyuzivani AI na VSB-TUO' (TUO_LEG_24_002), with version B dated 27 April 2026.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Current official Czech AI rules document found in VSB-TUO managed documents.

citation.citationTitle: Pravidla a doporuceni pro vyuzivani AI na VSB-TUO
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: VSB-TUO's AI rules set framework principles for AI tool use and apply to VSB-TUO devices and accounts, and to private devices when university data or outputs for VSB-TUO are processed.
artifacts.normalizedValue: AI rules scope includes university devices/accounts and private devices when processing university data or VSB-TUO outputs.

citation.citationTitle: Pravidla a doporuceni pro vyuzivani AI na VSB-TUO
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: For education, creative activity, and research, VSB-TUO's AI rules say users must properly state when a result was achieved using AI, including the scope of use; presenting AI-generated outputs as one's own work is considered fraud.
artifacts.normalizedValue: AI use disclosure required for education, creative activity, and research; undisclosed AI output presented as own work is fraud.

citation.citationTitle: Pravidla a doporuceni pro vyuzivani AI na VSB-TUO
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: VSB-TUO's AI rules prohibit entering sensitive data, trade-secret data, unpublished research results, intellectual-property-protected data, or sensitive contractual partner data into AI tools, and say all inputs must comply with EU GDPR law.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Sensitive, confidential, unpublished research, IP-protected, and certain partner data must not be entered into AI tools; inputs must comply with GDPR.

citation.citationTitle: Pravidla a doporuceni pro vyuzivani AI na VSB-TUO
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: VSB-TUO's AI rules list permissible student AI uses such as study inspiration, concept explanation, presentation preparation, stylistic corrections, and data analysis or modelling within projects unless prohibited; they list impermissible uses including automatic thesis writing, generating exam test materials, and creating deepfake or unethical manipulative content.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Student AI use examples are allowed for support tasks unless prohibited; automatic thesis writing, exam test materials, and deepfake/unethical content are impermissible.

citation.citationTitle: Pravidla a doporuceni pro vyuzivani AI na VSB-TUO
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: For teachers, VSB-TUO's AI rules list impermissible uses including automated grading of professional papers, essays, projects, and qualification theses, correcting tests, and entering students' personal data such as names, results, or health status into AI tools.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Teachers may not use AI for listed automated grading/test correction tasks or enter student personal data into AI tools.

citation.citationTitle: Pravidla a doporuceni pro vyuzivani AI na VSB-TUO
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: VSB-TUO Central Library guidance describes AI-generated outputs as comparable to grey literature and advises users not to rely on a single source, model, or tool, but to cross-check AI responses against other models, web information, archived websites, and scientific perspectives.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Library AI/literature-search guidance advises cross-checking AI outputs and not relying on a single model, tool, or source.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Pro oblast vzdelavani, tvurci cinnosti a vyzkumu plati, ze pokud bylo jakehokoli vysledku dosazeno s vyuzitim AI, je uzivatel povinen tuto skutecnost radne uvest v souladu s citacnimi pravidly ... artifacts.notes: Narrow source-status claim supported by the current official Czech AI rules PDF and regulations index.
Scope claim avoids overstating student binding where the binding list names employees, selected agreement workers, and external collaborators.
Academic-integrity claim uses the source's own mandatory language and fraud characterization.
Privacy claim is directly tied to the source's list of impermissible AI inputs and GDPR sentence.
Student-use claim is scoped to examples listed in the AI rules and includes the source's 'unless prohibited' qualifier for project analysis/modelling.
Teaching claim is limited to impermissible uses explicitly listed for teachers.
Library claim is treated as official guidance, not a binding policy rule.

artifacts.title: VSB - Technical University of Ostrava AI Policy Source Review
artifacts.summary: Official VSB-TUO sources include the university managed-regulations index, the current Czech version B AI rules PDF TUO_LEG_24_002, and an official Central Library literature-search page with AI research guidance. Claims are staged as agent-reviewed candidates only and are not canonical.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The Czech version B PDF is treated as canonical because it is newer than the English version A PDF available from the same document identifier.
The Central Library source is official guidance for literature search and AI source checking; it is not treated as a formal university-wide policy.
Expired webinar pages and course-specific pages were rejected as policy evidence.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
